items.title: [正面] [h2] Context: I HATED slay the spire, and I genera...
items.summary:
[h2] Context: I HATED slay the spire, and I generally dislike the predatory nature of gachas. But I ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ LOVE this game. [/h2]

- Over 50 characters to collect, mix and match.
- Viable build flexibility is through the roof.
- The same characters can fulfill multiple roles which means there are no hard limits on how to pair characters together so long as the required roles are fulfilled. The biggest green flag of this healthy matchmaking is how the leaderboards show the top endgame players using quite a huge variety of teams in different ways.
- Decent art and animations.
- The most generous f2p gacha system in existence. Games just ♥♥♥♥♥ out silver all the time and guarantee rates are great. The 2nd anniversary rewards alone were 200+ premium pulls.
- Your ears are going to have a field day here. This soundtrack is obsessively top quality.
- Game has TWO mechanics to deal with death. Death resistance is a chance to resist a fatal hit with 1 hp. When that fails, emergency gnosis lets you consume 1 out of 5 tokens to fully restore your entire team's health and ultimates, 1 token recharges for free daily. Money has no impact on either and both work in all pve content.
- The combination of the aformentioned incredible soundtrack and death disregardation, transforms some of these bosses into really tense, lengthy exhilirating experiences, and actually captures some of that SUPERB satisfaction from conquering an extreme tribulation you typically only get from soulslikes and endgame mmo raids.
- The progression pace and spread of appropriate content will last you quite a while. I have completed arc 1 of the story only after ~150 hours played. (And I skipped the majority of cutscenes lmao)
- Game lets you test try any character in the game without cost and without limit.
- You can borrow one character from the community or your followers/friends list in some of the modes. This allows you to tackle much higher difficulty content than you would otherwise be able to.
- There's a replay mode which lets you watch any clear of any boss from anyone. This is very useful to let you see in detail what teams the community used for whatever content you may be having trouble with. 
- Doesn't suffer from infinite menus and clicking simulator that gachas are infamous for. It exists, but not as sporadic.
- There's only 1 daily objective, to play any 50 cards, which can be completed in a couple of minutes.
- The entire soundtrack, art and lore of the game is accessible in the gallery. You are able to set a lot of tracks and wallpapers/animations as menu background.
- There's decent 1v1 pvp in this game. Fully equalized, pve progression completely disabled (aside from not being able to use any characters you haven't unlocked in pve). And completely separate balance and functionality of all characters. You can also watch replays of matches here.
- There's an identical version of this game on mobile with cross-progression.
- 10 GB size. Update sizes are in the megabytes instead of gigabytes.
- [b] IMPORTANT: [/b] You can now ignore anyone else complaining about the bad AI translation, as of the 2nd anniversary update it's been completely replaced.

Now for the bad stuff:
- The depicted themes and eldritch horror aspect of these stories are not for the faint of heart. The visual novel storytelling method is not for me but you can skip almost all of it.
- The depth of this game's mechanics and a lot of card text and descriptions can be overwhelming at first. You can't figure out how a lot of characters work at first glance most of the time.
- Very Duplicate-heavy design, some characters go from very bad to one of the best in the game with a couple of enlightens. However, unlike any other gacha you get one free duplicate for any unlocked character and wheel every month.
- No English voice acting of any kind anywhere. You are stuck with Japanese. Not a dealbreaker.
- This is a smaller community than most which means it's harder to find guides and helpful information. The official discord is your best bet.
- Difficulty spikes are not cool. You NEED to have a properly researched and built team by chapter 9.
- Balance is a bit all over the place. However, reworks of weaker characters happen and powercreep is not a major problem here in general. Again, research will be your best friend to navigate it.

Morimens stands on its own two feet without relying on oversexualized waifus like every other gacha game out there. It's not afraid to be confronting or difficult, but it's not constant unbearable RNG punishment unlike Slay the Spire. Death resistance and emergency gnosis go a long way to smooth out the experience at its roughest, and the amount of innovation, build flexibility, reward, art, and accessibility to all players here makes it stand very tall on the mountain of free-to-play greatness.

items.title: [正面] This game is like if Slay The Spire had better ani...
items.summary:
This game is like if Slay The Spire had better animations, better narrative, better characters with long-term progression and character building.

7/10
